prevalence.source: ESID/European primary immunodeficiency registry context; Orphanet
prevalence.trend: stable; improved detection and long-term outcomes with immunology networks

burden.dalyPerCapita: very low (rare) but largely preventable morbidity with immunoglobulin replacement
burden.mortalityRate: Low with regular IVIG/SCIG and infection prevention

vulnerablePopulations: patients with chronic lung disease complications
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Very low immunoglobulins, absent/low CD19+ B cells, recurrent bacterial infections; genetic confirmation (BTK)

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no routine screening; diagnosis via immunology pathways
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with regular IVIG/SCIG and structured follow-up

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Flow cytometry and genetics widely available; immunology referral networks established
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: IVIG/SCIG replacement, prompt antibiotics, lung monitoring, vaccination planning (avoid live vaccines in severe antibody deficiency)
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Ensuring adherence and transition to adult immunology care; cross-country access differences
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong access to IVIG/SCIG, immunology registries, and comprehensive follow-up
